name: The Blue Forest
description: Each spring, a dreamlike carpet of bluebells overtakes the forest floor.

name: Royal Greenhouses of Laeken
description: Lavish royal estate punctuated with heated greenhouses.

name: Le Cercueil (The Coffin)
description: The Devil’s own choice when he needs a drink in Brussels.

name: Comic Strip Route
description: This collection of over 50 murals celebrates Brussels' rich history as the self-proclaimed home of the "comic strip."

name: The Atomium
description: Climb inside an iron crystal magnified 165 billion times its normal size.

name: Gravensteen
description: This fairytale castle was built as a show of power but was nearly torn down thanks to its history of torture.

name: Reading Between The Lines
description: Part modern art project, part traditional chapel, this see-through church is only substantial from the right angle.

name: The Lucifernum
description: This former Masonic lodge has been transformed into part museum, part bar, all awesome.

name: Délirium Café
description: Cozy basement bar sports an inventory of over 2,000 beers.

name: The Musical Instrument Museum
description: Three floors of musical instruments coming from all corners of the world and hundreds of years of musical history.

name: The Belgian Centre for Comic Strip Art
description: From Tintin to the Smurfs, Europe's greatest graphic icons are celebrated in a grand Art Noveau gallery.

name: Musée de la Médecine
description: Medical history with an amazing, if frighting, collection of wax diseases on display.

name: Mini-Europe
description: Theme park featuring miniature replicas of European monuments.

name: Zinneke Pis
description: Meet the bronze beloved pet to a family of famous peeing statues.

name: Laeken Cemetery Crypt
description: This recently restored crypt was once plagued by liquefying coffins and exploding caskets.

name: Abbaye de Villers
description: A magical and peaceful ruined Cistercian abbey in what is now southern Belgium.

name: The Doomed City of Doel
description: This ghost town in Belgium will lose its street art when it ceases to exist.

name: Caves of Remouchamps
description: Hiding the longest subterranean river known in the world.

name: Cemetery Dieweg
description: Nature is slowly overtaking this disused cemetery on the outskirts of Brussels.

name: Basilica of the Holy Blood
description: Christ's blood allegedly preserved in a Belgian town.

name: Manneken Pis
description: A little sculpture with a large wardrobe.

name: Fort de la Chartreuse
description: This abandoned Belgian fortress is purposefully being left to nature.

name: Théâtre Royal de Toone
description: Drink among marionettes at this pub inside a historic puppet theater.

name: Black Tower
description: A fantastical 13th-century fortification sandwiched by a modern hotel in central Brussels.

name: Maison de la Bellone
description: This modern arts center is built around a gem of late-Baroque architecture.

name: Cook & Book
description: A wonderfully kitsch bookstore the size of a supermarket.

name: Tower of Eben-Ezer
description: A Belgian self-built tower inspired by the Bible and ancient civilizations.

name: La Balade des Gnomes
description: A fantastical bed and breakfast with magically unique rooms.

name: Frietmuseum
description: Potato-peeling mannequins, Inca art, and a dangling tuber mobile help trace the history of fries at this Belgian museum.

name: The Lucifer of Liège
description: A marble Lucifer hides behind the pulpit of this Gothic cathedral.

name: Nemo 33
description: One of the deepest indoor swimming pools in the world.

name: Sanctuary Wood Museum
description: A rare example of World War I trenches from the British front.

name: Commemoration of Peter the Great's Vomit
description: When you're a great Russian Tsar, even your vomit gets a statue.

name: Temple of Human Passions
description: This Greek temple, open only an hour a day, was the site of a war of tastes between an architect and an artist.

name: St. Anna's Tunnel
description: Rare wooden escalators lead deep down into an otherwise staggeringly monotonous tiled tunnel.

name: Dr. Guislain Museum
description: A Museum of Psychiatry in a still-operating mental institution.

name: Jeanneke Pis
description: Brussels' favorite peeing boy has a sister.

name: Klankenbos Sound Forest
description: Artwork tucked within the trees greets visitors with surreal sonic experiences.

name: Smedenpoort Skull
description: The bronzed skull of a beheaded traitor was affixed to the city gate in the 17th century.

name: The Zimmer Clock Tower and Museum
description: A 14th-century fortified keep showcases the masterpieces of an ingenious 20th-century clockmaker.

name: Musée des Égouts Bruxelles (Brussels Sewer Museum)
description: Head underground and take a tour of the city's working sewer system, and see the underground river Senne, which defined Brussels for centuries before being covered in the 19th century.

name: Torture Museum Bruges
description: Inside this former medieval prison is a collection of objects designed to inflict unbelievable pain and suffering on the human body and mind.

name: Cafe Beveren
description: This small corner bar is home to a rare 1930s dance organ that still plays tunes for one Euro.

name: Cycling Through Water
description: This bike path was sunk below water level for maximum wonder.

name: Het Bootje (The Little Boat)
description: The patron of this extravagant Art Nouveau confection insisted that nothing less than a boat should project from its facade.

name: The Yorkshire Trench
description: In an area where the remnants of WWI are discovered on a regular basis this preserved frontline trench may be the most amazing.

name: Royal Train Station
description: The Belgian royal family's private depot now stands abandoned and forgotten alongside the tracks.

name: Potato Vending Machine
description: Because sometimes you need to make a batch of frites even when the stores are all closed.

name: Cycling Through The Trees
description: This bike path traverses the treetops.

name: Bibliotheca Wittockiana
description: Only museum in the world devoted to bookbinding and book arts holds a permanent collection representing five centuries.

name: Hotel Casanus
description: Somewhere there is a giant who can no longer poop, but now travelers can stay inside his anus.

name: Kulminator
description: To enter this cluttered-yet-cozy sanctum of Belgian brews, ring the bell and tell the gatekeepers you've come “to taste beer.”

name: Vlooybergtoren
description: The "stairway to heaven" lookout tower was built to resist weather, arson, and the laws of physics.

name: Trolls of De Schorre Park
description: Take a leisurely walk through the park to find the seven giant troll sculptures hidden throughout the park.

name: Blegny-Mine
description: Descend into the tunnels to experience the life of a coal miner.

name: Werregarenstraat
description: This narrow Ghent alleyway is an officially sanctioned street art tunnel.

name: Nello and Patrasche
description: This statue dedicated to the story of a Flemish boy and his dog was relatively obscure in Belgium, but a cult sensation in Japan.

name: The Birthplace of 'In Flanders Fields'
description: The site where one of the most famous poems of World War I was composed is preserved to honor the legacy of the work.

name: Signal de Botrange
description: The highest point in Belgium is a staircase to nowhere.

name: Musée des Arbalétriers (Museum of Crossbowmen)
description: This charming, hidden guild museum holds some 700 years of crossbow history.

name: Antwerp Ruien
description: An open-air sewer system that was covered piece-by-piece over 300 years.

name: Fort Eben-Emael
description: This sprawling Belgian fort was once thought of as impenetrable but now it is simply an abandoned relic.

name: Plantin-Moretus Museum of Printing
description: Most prolific publishing house in the 1600s open to the public.

name: Museum of Radiology
description: Dummies recreate radiological discoveries from history.

name: Borders of Baarle-Nassau and Baarle-Hertog
description: A maze of crosses marks the jumbled divide between the Netherlands and Belgium.

name: Le Museé du Jouet (Toys Discovery Museum)
description: A huge old mansion full of thousands of toys, each there to be used and played with.

name: Grave of Antoine Michel Wemaer
description: The spectacular and eerie moss-covered grave site of 19th-century Belgian merchant.

name: Kemmel's Top Secret Command Bunker
description: A Cold War command center is hidden under an idyllic countryside.

name: Bueren Mountain
description: This giant urban staircase was built to keep the city's brave fighting men from getting waylaid in the red light district.

name: The Flashing Streetlights of Ai Nati Oggi
description: Each time a baby is born in Ghent, these streetlights flash in silent celebration.

name: Collégiale Sainte-Croix de Liège
description: A once-magnificent collegiate church is falling into ruin.

name: Verbeke Foundation
description: A collection of weird and wonderful art fills a former houseplant nursery.

name: Halve Maan Brewery Beer Pipeline
description: A beer pipeline runs beneath Bruges.

name: Cinéma Nova
description: This non-profit theatre in the heart of Brussels is dedicated to hosting independent films.

name: In Flanders Fields Museum
description: This memorial museum chronicles the apocalyptic destruction of the First World War.

name: National Museum of the Resistance
description: This museum is housed in an unassuming building that witnessed one of the most daring feats of satire in modern history.

name: 'Snow Whites' of Leuven
description: The Belgian library displays burned books that were salvaged from the fires of both world wars.

name: Everard t'Serclaes Monument
description: A shining memorial to a Belgian hero is said to bring luck to anyone who touches it.

name: Lumina Domestica
description: Explore 400,000 years of humans' "battle against darkness" in this offbeat but thorough lamp museum.

name: Gare De Montzen
description: Abandoned since 1998, this train station has become an oasis for urban explorers.

name: Fons Sapientiae
description: This whimsical statue dumps water on its head to symbolize knowledge flowing into his brain. Or maybe booze.

name: Hotel Kosmos
description: This old, abandoned hotel offers magnificent views.

name: Mercator Museum
description: Housing the groundbreaking maps and globes of Gerard Mercator.

name: Brabo Fountain
description: A man throwing a giant severed hand towers over Antwerp’s main square.

name: Elfde Gebod
description: According to this bar, God gave an “11th commandment” to Antwerp: Thou shalt drink beer.

name: Hand of Druon Antigoon
description: The legendary giant's severed hand sits on Antwerp's popular shopping street.

name: Museum of Carrots
description: This small window display is dedicated to the noble carrot.

name: Mundaneum
description: A visionary precursor to the Internet made of index cards.

name: Garderobe Manneken Pis
description: Belgium’s famous peeing statue has worn many hats (and coats, and pants), which are on display at this small museum.

name: Genk Sundial Park
description: This collection of solar timekeepers includes the world's first digital sundial.

name: Musée Hergé (Hergé Museum)
description: Tintin's creator is celebrated in an immersive museum that's an astounding work of modern art.

name: Sourdough Library
description: Burbling away in refrigerators are 105 sourdough starters from around the world.

name: Carnival of Binche
description: The tiny Belgian town's ancient version of Mardi Gras involves eerie wax masks, sticks for warding off evil, and ostrich plume hats.

name: 'Totem'
description: A giant bug impaled on a 75-foot-tall needle.

name: The Guardhouse Monkey
description: Petting this mysterious iron monkey supposedly brings good luck.

name: Museum of Erotics and Mythology
description: A hidden museum focusing on the sexual aspects of anthropology.

name: Museum Vleeshuis
description: This former guildhall with a bacon-like exterior is now home to a museum covering 600 years of Antwerp's musical history.

name: Menin Gate Memorial
description: A moving memorial to the fallen and missing from WWI trenches.

name: Westvleteren
description: Stocking up on the world's best beer requires a pilgrimage to a monastery drive-through.

name: James Ensor House
description: The former home of a trailblazing surrealist is still filled with haunting dolls and grim paintings.

name: C-Mine
description: What was once a bustling mining installation is now a bustling hub of contemporary art.

name: House Bouchoute
description: The compass on its front was a handy tool for 17th-century merchants who needed to know the direction of the wind.

name: Volkssterrenwacht Armand Pien
description: Learn about stars, weather patterns, or just enjoy the panoramic view of Ghent.

name: The Procession of the Holy Blood
description: This gruesome biblical parade celebrates the one day a year that a bit of Jesus' blood turns to liquid.

name: René Magritte Museum
description: A lovely biographical museum in the artist's home of 24 years.

name: The Canada War Museum of Adegem, Belgium
description: A museum celebrating the Canadian forces that liberated Belgium from the Nazis replete with battlefield miniatures and multiple Gardens.

name: The Charles de Gaulle Saxophone Bridge
description: A whimsical bridge uses saxophones to pay tribute to two great men and the European Union.

name: Lieteberg Blotevoetenpad
description: No shoes allowed on this trail, which is designed to be experienced barefoot.

name: Strépy-Thieu Boat Lift
description: The world's tallest boat lift stands as a massive piece of industrial tourism.

name: Smallest Gothic Window in Bruges
description: You can spot this teeny tiny stained-glass window from the Bonifacius bridge.

name: Castle Nieuwenhoven
description: This Belgian palace was long the domain of priests and aristocrats but is now being turned into an art commune.

name: Royal Museum for Central Africa Natural History Collections
description: A fascinating and enormous collection of natural history exhibits stained by a dark colonial legacy.

name: Hopital Notre-Dame à la Rose
description: One of Belgium's oldest hospitals saw patients from the 13th to 20th centuries before becoming a museum.

name: 'Jonas'
description: An unsettling sculpture of a boy hanging out in a street in Ghent has been baffling visitors for years.

name: Centrale Begraafplaats Brugge Assebroek
description: The oldest cemetery in Belgium.

name: Lange Wapper Statue
description: This legendary giant, born from vegetables, terrorized townspeople and cheated local children in games.

name: The Whisperer
description: This interactive sculpture allows visitors to leave a message in case a friend passes by.

name: Bandundu Water Jazz Band
description: A jazz band formed by African water animals in the heart of Flemish Brabant.

name: Sanctuary Wood Cemetery
description: In the shadow of a former battleground, the resting place for nearly 2,000 soldiers who died in World War I.

name: Hôtel Solvay
description: This historic Art Nouveau townhouse was the former home of the son of a famous chemical inventor and industrialist.

name: Birthplace of Audrey Hepburn
description: One of Hollywood's most iconic actresses was born in an upscale Brussels neighborhood in 1929.

name: National Museum of the Playing Card
description: Relics from as far back as the 16th century are on display in a city known for its playing card industry.

name: Grotto of St Anthony of Padua
description: This collection of lurid religious statues tell the tales of a saint who stood down the devil.

name: Museum of the National Bank of Belgium
description: One of the oldest bank museums in Europe showcases unusual currencies of the world and other money-related miscellanea.

name: International Regatta of Bathtubs
description: This annual Belgian water race attracts bathtub captains from all over the world.

name: The House with Two Numbers
description: This residence straddles a border between Belgium and the Netherlands.

name: 'Searching for Utopia'
description: A massive, gleaming sea turtle with a rider atop its shell looks out over the horizon.

name: 'Cement Truck'
description: Intricate metal sculpture that's half gothic cathedral, half construction equipment.

name: The Devil's Pants
description: A piece of stonework relief commemorates the site where the Devil's rage, and odd apparel, destroyed a house.

name: Caesar's Tree
description: A taxus-tree to which Julius Caesar reputedly tied his horse.

name: Halte Royal d'Ardenne
description: The private train station King Leopold II built for the Belgian royal family now lies abandoned.

name: St. Rumbold's Cathedral
description: The tallest church in Mechelen might have been the tallest in the world if not for a lack of funds.

name: Vennbahn Cycleway
description: Walk or bike along a narrow strip of Belgium sandwiched by German territory.

name: Cannonball in St. Nicholas Church
description: This cannonball has been lodged in the church wall since a 1695 bombardment destroyed the city center.

name: Autoworld
description: From motorcycles to firetrucks and sports cars to horse-drawn carriages, this Belgian collection goes beyond rare cars.

name: Walraversijde
description: Rediscovered in 1992, this abandoned medieval village once flourished by fishing and piracy.

name: Paep Thoon
description: Banished from town, this local jester concocted a clever return.

name: Damme Canal
description: Also known as Napoleon's Canal, this waterway was created on the orders of the general, and construction was interrupted by Waterloo.

name: Belfry of Tournai
description: After the construction of a nearby cathedral obstructed the view, the oldest belfry in Belgium was more than doubled in height.

name: Ruckersplaats Hands Monument
description: Hidden along this old street in Antwerp is a surreal monument of a bronze arm sprouting four hands.

name: Semini Sculpture
description: This heavily worn bas-relief was once an honored fertility symbol.

name: Wiertz Museum
description: The former home and studio of Belgian artist Antoine Wiertz is now a museum dedicated to his work.

name: Geel, Belgium
description: A pilgrimage site where people ask Saint Dymphna to watch over those who are not of sound mind.

name: The Tomb of Mr. Hawarden at Malmédy
description: A gender-bending antihero's grave, where lies the inspiration for both book and film.

name: Bois du Cazier
description: This former coal mine, now a fascinating museum, was the site of Belgium's worst mining disaster.

name: Madame Chapeau Sculpture
description: A bronze statue of the fictional character nicknamed "Mrs. Hat" from a beloved Belgian comedy.

name: Vladslo German Military Cemetery
description: Visitors to this Belgium graveyard are greeted by the grieving stone parents of the young men lost to war.

name: The Inventor of Time Travel Plaque
description: Above the door of a house, a plaque claims to commemorate the future living place of the inventor of time travel.

name: Ruins of Fort de Knocke
description: The foundations of a forgotten fortress, wedged in between two streams.

name: Leuven Mini City
description: A tiny metropolis resides inside this student housing complex.

name: Jeanne Dielman Mural
description: A mural recalls a fictional woman who once lived at 23 Quai du Commerce, 1080 Brussels.

name: Tour Cybernétique (Cybernetic Tower)
description: Nicolas Schöffer's massive interactive sculpture was assembled in 1961 and brought back online in 2016.

name: Museum voor Dierkunde
description: This 19th-century zoological collection has withstood the test of time.

name: Amandine Museum Ship
description: The last Oostende trawler to fish off Iceland is now a museum.

name: McAuliffe Memorial and Sherman Tank
description: Dedicated to the American general whose reply to a German demand for surrender was simply "Nuts!"

name: Den Spauwer
description: Known as "The Spewer," this bizarre fountain has an even stranger urban legend behind it.

name: ‘Don Quixote and Sancho Panza’
description: A largely overlooked statue that may commemorate the first publication of 'Don Quixote' outside of Spain.

name: Hof ter Saksen
description: The ruins of a castle sat decaying in this park for years before a massive renovation restored this area to its past beauty.

name: Tilleul de Gérouville
description: A 600-year-old elm tree with a built-in seat and thatched roof.

name: De Kangxi-Verbiest Hemelglobe (Kangxi-Verbiest Star Globe)
description: A replica of a 17th-century map of the stars stands in the courtyard of a Belgian university.

name: Largest Paving Stone in Belgium
description: This huge limestone slab is considered the largest paving stone in Belgium and a relic of the earliest modern paving in Brussels.

name: Heksensteen
description: A curious stone in a public square commemorates the victims of witch-burning.

name: Neuhaus Chocolate Shop
description: The original shop where the iconic Belgian praline was invented.

name: 'De Koeieschieter' ('Cow Shooter')
description: A small statue commemorates a centuries-old event that still hangs over the people of Leuven.

name: The Migrating House
description: Not many houses move across borders, this one did it twice.

name: The Hanging Gardens of Leuven
description: A monumental toilet that is also host to rare plants.

name: 'Primeval Atom'
description: An installation that makes understanding the size of the universe a walk in the park.

name: Zendstation MN7
description: A reconstructed World War I-era communications array that was once hidden in plain sight.

name: Duffelcoat Statue
description: The birthplace of the titular coat and related products celebrates its claim to fame with a statue of a coat.

name: 'Renée'
description: This statue of a studious young woman stands at the site of Leuven's first college to accept female students.

name: Cointe Observatory
description: This Belgian observatory bears a striking resemblance to Futurama's Planet Express building.

name: Gabrielle Petit Statue
description: Belgium's national heroine turned spy sacrificed her life to combat German occupation.

name: Le Caillou-qui-bique
description: Local lore surrounds this unusual stone.

name: Collegiate Church of St. Gertrude
description: The patron saint of cats founded this stunning Romanesque church in the 7th century.

name: Charmille of Haut-Marêt
description: Europe's longest Hornbeam tunnel.

name: 'De Pungelaer'
description: A tribute to smugglers and the Butter Wars.

name: Hitler's Bunker in Bruly-de-Pesche
description: This bunker site once briefly used by Hitler now features locals’ accounts of German occupation.

name: Brian Johnson Statue
description: Forget the hearse ‘cause he’s forever immortalized in a Belgian city where AC/DC performed a secret gig that pushed them forward.

name: Nike Laakdal Wind Park
description: Six turbines towering over a wide road for passersby.

name: ‘The Container’
description: In the middle of a Brussels roundabout, a cargo container balancing on its edge.

name: Bow of HMS Vindictive
description: A piece of the front end of a British cruiser that was used as a block ship in World War I.

name: Miniatures of Isaac Cordal
description: Tiny sculptures hidden in plain sight miniatures in Namur.

name: Sarcophagus of Chrodoara
description: Since its accidental discovery in 1977, this sarcophagus has been described as "the greatest masterpiece of Merovingian sculpture north of the Alps."

name: Leuven Historical Measures
description: Though they might seem like arbitrary scratches in the wall, these were once official measures of length.

name: Vlaeykensgang
description: A beautifully preserved and hidden 16th-century alleyway, tucked behind an unassuming door.

name: Les Cinq Tombes (The Five Graves)
description: A unique collection of five Roman burial grounds right next to the road.

name: Geographic Center of Belgium
description: This metal monument marking the middle of Belgium represents three regions coming together to form one country.

name: The Heart of André Grétry
description: After a long legal battle, the heart of the famous composer is finally enthroned in the city of his birth.

name: Gilliot & Roelants Tile Museum
description: The only museum dedicated to the tile production for which the Rupel region was renowned.

name: The Glass Chapel
description: The unlikely Art Deco shrine to marbrite, a vanished glass.

name: Musée du Coeur
description: This section of the Art and History Museum's collections at the Cinquantenaire explores the symbolism of the heart in Western society.

name: Centre de l'Europe des 15 (Geographical Center of the 15-member European Union)
description: This site used to mark the heart of the EU—now it's crowned with a striking glass sculpture and a buried secret underneath.

name: Middelpunt van Leuven
description: This unassuming stone bollard is a centuries-old marker denoting the center of the city.

name: ‘Arc Majeur’ by Bernard Venet
description: This work of art, which took 35 years to complete, has a motorway running through it.

name: Verviers Station's Comical Hare and Tortoise Bas-relief
description: A humorous work of art making fun of the station's very long construction time.

name: Heilige Geestcollege
description: Where George Lemaître discovered the expanding nature of the universe.

name: Schaerbeek Beer Museum
description: A collection of 2000 bottles of Belgium's favorite drink.

name: Seven Stars Pavilion
description: A 17th-century heptagonal pavilion that was used for stargazing.

name: Notre-Dame des Récollets
description: This statue of the Virgin and Child is said to have miraculously changed its position during the 1692 earthquake.

name: Dronkenput (Drunken Well)
description: A tilted water tank turned tourist attraction.

name: Goblet d’Alviella Mausoleum
description: Freemasonry’s universal creed etched in limestone.

name: Middelpunt van Vlaanderen
description: The geographic midpoint of the Dutch-speaking part of Belgium.

name: ‘La Grande Assiette Brisée’ (‘The Large Broken Plate’)
description: This surprising work of art reminds us that La Louvière was once home to one of Europe’s most famous stoneware factories.

name: ‘Dogs Out of God’s House’ Inscription
description: Old signage above the doors of a church with an amusing backstory.

name: Crypt of Father Damien
description: A chapel quietly preserves the remains of Father Damien, who devoted his life to caring for leprosy patients.

name: Chapelle Saint-Mort
description: The 6th-century saint is often invoked for tooth pain and young children who experience problems walking.

name: ‘Djoseph et Françwès’
description: A pair of iconic characters from the local newspaper argue over pet snails.

name: Six Fontaines
description: A Belgian town divided its water supply into six troughs, each with a specific use.

name: Vloethemveld
description: Formerly a munitions depot and World War II POW camp, now a nature reserve.

name: Neutral Moresnet
description: This little patch of Europe remained neutral for more than a century because of a mine.

name: ‘Moment - Point Zéro’
description: This conceptual artwork might be the most disappointing attraction in Brussels.

name: La Fresque des Wallons
description: A trompe-l'oeil fresco celebrating 250 Walloon figures who left their mark on the region.

name: ‘Bonhomme de Fer’ (‘The Iron Man’)
description: This human-shaped statue is actually a geodesic station.

name: World's Largest Wheelbarrow
description: Though Guinness took away its record, Romedenne’s 1998 wheelbarrow remains the biggest of them all.

name: Quartier des Carmes
description: This is a surprising Art Deco district in a city with a history dating back to the 17th and 18th centuries.

name: The Ceiling of the Guard Room at Modave Castle
description: The largest carved family tree in Europe is 135 square meters.

name: St. Paul's Cathedral Flood Gauge
description: A stone record of five floods inside Liège’s cathedral.

name: Rocks of Marche-les-Dames
description: Where a cross marks the spot King Albert I’s body was found on 17 February 1934.

name: Le Cheval Bayard
description: This unfinished sculpture, once loathed by locals, ultimately earned their affection.

name: Power Plant IM
description: The vast chamber inside this abandoned cooling tower looks like something out of a sci-fi dystopia.

name: Giant Clothespin Sculpture
description: Sculpture designed by Turkish artist and professor Mehmet Ali Uysal.

name: Secret Garden of Bruges
description: You would never come across this tiny green oasis if you didn't know it existed.

name: The Grottoes of Folx-les-Caves
description: Ancient man-made caves underneath small town Belgium.

name: Sanatorium du Basil
description: An early 20th-century sanatorium, long abandoned and now being reclaimed by nature.

name: Sandworm
description: An undulating wicker beast looms peacefully atop a sand dune in Belgium.

name: Dadipark
description: This Belgian theme park began as a refuge for bored church children and was closed down when a boy lost an arm.

name: Jacques Chocolate Museum
description: Museum covers every step of the chocolate-making process, from cultivation to the finished product.

name: Schedelhof
description: This impressive collection of skulls and bones was gathered by an ex-zookeeper who took advantage of a pig plague.

name: Vlindertuin
description: A garden of flying flowers.

name: La Pasionaria
description: This enormous steel tube is the biggest speaking horn in the world.

name: The Passenger
description: A 141-foot wave of crashing lumber guards the entrance to the Grand Place in Mons.
